title: Vriesea lineata
taxonomy: Vriesea lineata is classified as a species of bromeliad within the family Bromeliaceae. It belongs to the genus Vriesea, which comprises over 200 species of epiphytic plants. The genus Vriesea is part of the subfamily Tillandsioideae. This classification places Vriesea lineata within the monocot group of flowering plants, characterized by having one cotyledon in their seedlings. The Bromeliaceae family is a diverse group of plants with many species native to the Americas.

side_effects: Vriesea lineata is a low-maintenance epiphyte, but as with many plants, handling them improperly can lead to some side effects. Overwatering or underwatering can cause root rot, which can be detrimental to the plant's health. In addition, the sap of the Vriesea lineata plant may cause skin irritation in some individuals, especially if they have sensitive skin. Allergic reactions to the plant's sap can range from mild redness to severe blistering. It's essential to handle the plant with care and follow proper care instructions to minimize potential side effects.
